title: auto-mitamae orchestrator hosts.json
description: Fleet host registry consumed by orchestrator.sh (Phase 2b). Each entry carries connection info plus an optional ct_id (Phase B-2) used by the lazy credential re-seed step.

host.description: IPv4 address — orchestrator SSHes to this directly (no DNS lookup at apply time)

user.description: SSH login user (typically root for LXCs)

role.description: mitamae role file path, e.g. pve/lxc-monitoring.rb

label.description: Prometheus metric label and Grafana panel grouping key — must match contracts/devices.json device key

ct_id.description: Phase B-2: PVE LXC container ID. Set for LXC hosts (orchestrator's ensure_creds step calls bootstrap-lxc-creds <ct_id> on auth failure). Set to null for bare-metal / EC2 hosts where the credential re-seed step is skipped.

canary.description: Phase 3 stability rollout: if true, orchestrator applies this host BEFORE iterating the rest of the fleet. A cookbook failure on a canary aborts the cycle without touching non-canary hosts, containing fleet-wide propagation of cookbook bugs. Recovery is automatic: next commit fixes the bug, drift-checker observes the new SHA, canary apply succeeds, fleet rollout proceeds. Default: false. Pick a low-impact host (dev workstation, throwaway) — service LXCs should not be canary.
